agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"API Gateway","to":"OrderService","status":"correct","issue":"Trace ID properly propagated via X-Trace-ID header"},{"from":"OrderService","to":"Kafka","status":"correct","issue":"Trace ID correctly embedded in Kafka message headers"},{"from":"Kafka","to":"FulfillmentWorker","status":"broken","issue":"FulfillmentWorker ignores trace ID in Kafka message headers and generates a new one — this severs the distributed trace, making it impossible to correlate an order request with its fulfillment"},{"from":"FulfillmentWorker","to":"NotificationService","status":"broken","issue":"HTTP call made without any trace ID header — NotificationService starts a completely independent trace"},{"from":"OrderService","to":"AuditService","status":"broken","issue":"HTTP call made without propagating X-Trace-ID — audit records cannot be correlated to the originating request"},{"from":"FulfillmentWorker","to":"AuditService","status":"broken","issue":"HTTP call made without any trace ID — audit records from fulfillment are orphaned from both the original request AND the fulfillment worker's own trace"}],"contract_compliance":"Only 2 of 6 inter-service links correctly propagate trace context. The implicit contract that X-Trace-ID flows end-to-end is violated at every boundary after Kafka.","data_flow_integrity":"The system produces three disconnected trace segments: (1) API Gateway → OrderService → Kafka publish, (2) FulfillmentWorker with its own ID, (3) NotificationService with its own ID. AuditService entries are completely orphaned. An on-call engineer investigating a failed order would need to manually correlate logs across all three segments using business identifiers like order ID — if those are even logged consistently."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["FulfillmentWorker logs use a locally-generated trace ID that cannot be correlated to the upstream request","NotificationService logs are completely disconnected from the originating order flow","AuditService log entries from OrderService vs FulfillmentWorker cannot be traced back to the same customer request","No evidence of consistent business-context fields (e.g. order_id, customer_id) that could serve as a fallback correlation key"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"Per-service counters and histograms exist, but no cross-service or end-to-end metrics","gaps":["No end-to-end order latency metric (API Gateway receive → NotificationService sent)","No cross-service SLO tracking (e.g. p99 order-to-fulfillment time)","No Kafka consumer lag metrics mentioned for FulfillmentWorker","No error rate correlation across service boundaries","No metric tracking trace propagation failures or orphaned traces"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"Broken — effective tracing covers only the first two services (API Gateway → OrderService). The remaining three services operate with independent, uncorrelated traces.","gaps":["FulfillmentWorker must extract X-Trace-ID from Kafka message headers instead of generating a new one","FulfillmentWorker must propagate the extracted trace ID to all outbound HTTP calls (NotificationService, AuditService)","OrderService must propagate X-Trace-ID on its HTTP call to AuditService","Without these fixes, no single trace can show the full lifecycle of an order"]},"debugging_capability":"Severely compromised. At 3am, an engineer investigating 'order placed but notification never sent' would face: (1) find the trace in API Gateway/OrderService, (2) hit a dead end at Kafka, (3) have to grep FulfillmentWorker logs by order_id (hoping it's logged), (4) then grep NotificationService and AuditService independently. This turns a 5-minute trace lookup into a 30+ minute manual log archaeology exercise."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"FulfillmentWorker crashes after consuming Kafka event but before calling NotificationService","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Without end-to-end trace correlation, there is no way to detect that an order was fulfilled but the customer was never notified. Add a reconciliation check or dead-letter queue with alerting."},{"scenario":"AuditService is down while OrderService and FulfillmentWorker attempt calls","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Unclear if callers have retry/fallback. If audit writes are fire-and-forget, orders could complete with no audit trail. Determine if audit is critical-path or best-effort and handle accordingly."},{"scenario":"Kafka consumer lag spikes causing delayed fulfillment","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"No consumer lag metrics mentioned. Add lag monitoring with alerts so the team knows when fulfillment is falling behind before customers complain."},{"scenario":"Investigating a customer complaint spanning all five services","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Currently requires manual correlation across three disconnected trace trees. Fix trace propagation to enable single-query investigation."}],"retry_config":"Not specified for any inter-service call. FulfillmentWorker→NotificationService and *→AuditService HTTP calls have unknown retry behavior.","timeout_config":"Not specified. Unknown timeout settings for HTTP calls between FulfillmentWorker→NotificationService, OrderService→AuditService, FulfillmentWorker→AuditService.","circuit_breakers":"No circuit breakers mentioned. If AuditService goes down, both OrderService and FulfillmentWorker could be impacted depending on call semantics.","graceful_degradation":"No degradation strategy described. Unknown whether failures in NotificationService or AuditService block order processing or are handled independently."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":false,"rollback_plan":"No rollback plan assessed — the broken observability means even determining whether a rollback is needed would be difficult since end-to-end health cannot be measured.","monitoring_coverage":"Per-service metrics exist but are insufficient. No end-to-end SLOs, no cross-service dashboards, no Kafka lag monitoring. The team can tell if individual services are up but cannot answer 'are orders flowing correctly from placement to notification?'","on_call_documentation":"Insufficient. Without end-to-end tracing, runbooks cannot reference a single trace ID for investigation. Any incident spanning the Kafka boundary requires manual log correlation across disconnected trace segments.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["FulfillmentWorker: extract and propagate X-Trace-ID from Kafka message headers","OrderService: propagate X-Trace-ID on HTTP calls to AuditService","FulfillmentWorker: propagate trace ID on HTTP calls to NotificationService and AuditService"],"infra_provisioning":["Cross-service dashboard tracking end-to-end order flow","Kafka consumer lag monitoring and alerting"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":false,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No database changes involved in this assessment"},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"Distributed tracing is broken at 4 of 6 service boundaries","reason":"FulfillmentWorker discards the upstream trace ID from Kafka headers and generates a new one. Neither FulfillmentWorker nor OrderService propagate trace IDs to downstream HTTP calls (NotificationService, AuditService). This makes end-to-end debugging impossible and incident response dangerously slow. An on-call engineer cannot follow a single request through the system.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"integration_risk","issue":"AuditService receives calls from two services with no trace correlation","reason":"Both OrderService and FulfillmentWorker call AuditService without propagating trace IDs. Audit records for the same logical order will appear as unrelated entries, undermining the audit trail's purpose and potentially creating compliance risk.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Trace propagation gaps are clearly defined in the system description","The 4 broken inter-service trace links are unambiguous","Per-service metrics coverage is confirmed present"],"low_confidence_areas":["Retry, timeout, and circuit breaker configurations are not specified — may exist but were not described","Whether business-context fields (order_id) are logged consistently across services is unknown","Error handling semantics for AuditService and NotificationService failures are unspecified"],"assumptions":["The system is expected to support end-to-end request tracing as a design intent, given that API Gateway generates and propagates trace IDs","AuditService is architecturally important enough that losing audit records is unacceptable","No additional correlation mechanisms (e.g. consistent order_id logging) exist beyond what was described"]}}
